phishing_hunting_queries.title: Users Who Clicked Phishing Links Today
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Identify all internal users who clicked a link leading to a known phishing domain or GoPhish tracking URL within the last 24 hours.
phishing_hunting_queries.kql: event.category:network AND network.direction:outbound AND url.domain:("phishing-lab.local" OR "phish" OR "credential-harvest") AND NOT url.domain:"company.local" AND @timestamp:[now-24h TO now]
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Pivot on user.name to check all subsequent activity. Look for auth events or lateral movement within 30 minutes of the click.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: Credential Submissions to External Sites
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Detect HTTP POST requests containing credential-like field names sent to non-corporate domains.
phishing_hunting_queries.kql: http.request.method:POST AND http.request.body.content:("password" OR "passwd" OR "credential" OR "login") AND NOT destination.domain:"company.local" AND network.direction:outbound AND @timestamp:[now-7d TO now]
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Cross-reference submitting users with recent phishing campaign targets. Treat any match as a credential compromise incident.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: Email Links Pointing to IP Addresses
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Find emails containing hyperlinks that point directly to IP addresses rather than domain names — strong indicator of phishing.
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Extract the IP and run reputation check. Block the IP at the email gateway. Notify all recipients.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: Executable File Downloads via Email-Referred Links
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Detect file downloads with executable MIME types where the referer header indicates the request originated from an email client or webmail.
phishing_hunting_queries.kql: http.response.mime_type:("application/octet-stream" OR "application/exe" OR "application/x-msdownload" OR "application/zip") AND http.request.referrer:("mail." OR "webmail." OR "outlook." OR "mail.google.com") AND @timestamp:[now-24h TO now]
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Hash the downloaded file, check against VirusTotal. Quarantine endpoint immediately if file is malicious. Preserve memory snapshot.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: Multiple Users Accessing the Same Suspicious URL
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Find external URLs that multiple internal users have visited, which may indicate a phishing campaign email was broadly distributed.
phishing_hunting_queries.kql: NOT url.domain:"company.local" AND network.direction:outbound AND @timestamp:[now-4h TO now]
phishing_hunting_queries.aggregation: terms on url.full with min_doc_count:3 and sub-aggregation of unique user.name count
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Any external URL with 3+ distinct users in a short window warrants immediate investigation. Pull the email that distributed the link.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: After-Hours Email Link Clicks
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Detect proxy hits from email-referred links occurring outside business hours (before 08:00 or after 20:00), which may indicate compromised account automation.
phishing_hunting_queries.kql: http.request.referrer:("mail." OR "webmail." OR "outlook.") AND NOT url.domain:"company.local" AND (hour_of_day < 8 OR hour_of_day > 20) AND @timestamp:[now-7d TO now]
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Legitimate after-hours clicks do happen, but combined with suspicious domain, escalate for review. Check if the user account was active at that time.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: New Domain First Seen in Email Links
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Identify email links pointing to domains that have not been seen in email traffic in the past 30 days — new domains are frequently registered for phishing.
phishing_hunting_queries.kql: email.url:* AND event.category:email AND NOT email.url_domain_seen_before:true AND @timestamp:[now-24h TO now]
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Run whois on the domain. Domains registered within 30 days are high-risk. Block preemptively if content is suspicious.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: High Volume Emails from New External Sender
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Detect external senders that have sent more than 10 emails to internal recipients within 1 hour and have no prior sending history.
phishing_hunting_queries.kql: event.category:email AND email.direction:inbound AND email.from_domain_first_seen:true AND @timestamp:[now-1h TO now]
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Cross-reference sending domain reputation. If domain is newly registered or has poor reputation, quarantine all emails from the sender.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: URLs with Suspicious or High-Risk TLDs in Email
phishing_hunting_queries.description: Find emails containing links using TLDs commonly associated with phishing and free domain abuse.
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Suspicious TLDs alone are not conclusive but raise the risk score. Combine with other indicators. Consider blocking .tk and .ml at the gateway entirely.

phishing_hunting_queries.title: Proxy Activity After Phishing Click (Pivot Investigation)
phishing_hunting_queries.description: For a user known to have clicked a phishing link, retrieve all their subsequent proxy activity within 2 hours to identify C2, data exfiltration, or lateral movement.
phishing_hunting_queries.time_range: Custom: 2 hours from phishing click timestamp
phishing_hunting_queries.investigation_notes: Replace TARGET_USER and CLICK_TIMESTAMP with values from the GoPhish campaign results. Look for unusual domains, large data transfers, or connections to known C2 infrastructure.
